King Philip's new identity is led by sophomore Calleigh Brady, junior Grace Leehan-Allen,and freshman Laney O'Shea on the blue line, and junior netminder Kiki Lynch, who has allowed just six goals through six games.
After winning three consecutive games by one-goal margins, the Warriors secured their most impressive victory to date in a 3-0 win over No. 20 Canton on Saturday.

"It's tribute to all the backcheckers, defenders, goaltending, you name it, they're all just coming together and playing as one," Assad said. "It's a different brand of hockey that thev're putting out this year"
a ■Canton got of to a 3-0-1start under first-year coach Marissa Massaro, prior to dropping games this week to King Phillip and Notre Dame (Hingham).
One key weapon for the Bulldogs is sophomore forward Lauren Raffetto – who, when
she is not playing top-line minutes for the girls'hockey team, is one of the state's best cros-country runners.
Raffetto finished 13th in the MIAA Division 2Meet of Champions race in November, and she also owns Canton's 5K school record time by 45 seconds.
Raffetto scored a goal in Canton's 3-1 victory over No. 12 Nauset on Thursday, and her crosscountry skill-set has proven to be an asset.

"Her endurance is incredible. She can play as many minutes as I possibly need," Massaro said."I think at one point[against Nauset], she played three minut straiht and ot of the e and wasn't tired. She's skilled and she's aggressive in the best way possible, and she can go and go and never worry about running out of steam."
